query: I want to write a new MCP server in Python that wraps our internal incident-database API. It'll expose tools like search_incidents, get_incident, and list_recent. What do I need to bake in from day one so it'll pass a pre-trust audit when someone installs it?
expected_behavior: Recommends declaring an SPDX license in pyproject.toml AND committing a matching LICENSE file at the repo root before the first publish

Names the six-check pre-trust audit (license, source review, network egress, version pin, secret handling, tool subset) and frames each check as a design constraint to bake in BEFORE first publish, not after
Recommends an explicit tool-subset declaration — only the three named tools (search_incidents, get_incident, list_recent), no over-broad catch-alls, no eval-style dynamic dispatch
